Title: ARABIDOPSIS DEHISCENCE ZONE POLYGALACTURONASE 1 (ADPG1) releases latent defense signals in stems with reduced lignin content
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Polygalacturonase; glycoside hydrolase family 28 pectin-degrading enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Pectin degradation / cell wall remodeling associated with defense signal release
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Apple pectin; polygalacturonic acid; RG-I; RG-II
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes pectic galacturonan linkages in cell wall pectin to release soluble pectin-derived elicitor-active fragments
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Pectin remodeling step that releases latent defense elicitors from the wall
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/release activation of latent pectin-derived defense signals
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control step for elicitor release and PR gene induction in reduced-lignin stems
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Normally expressed during anther and silique dehiscence / cell separation processes
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Ectopically highly expressed in inflorescence stems, especially xylem, of HCT-RNAi and ccr1 plants
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Inflorescence stem cell wall water extracts; vascular/xylem-derived stem tissues
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Induces PR1, PR2, and PR10 defense gene expression; activates defense signaling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Reduced lignin backgrounds caused by HCT down-regulation or ccr1 loss of function; water extraction; PGase/arabinanase pretreatment affected activity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for release/activation of latent pectin-derived elicitors and for ectopic PR gene induction in reduced-lignin Arabidopsis stems
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ADPG1 was one of the most strongly induced cell-wall genes in both HCT-RNAi and ccr1 stems. 2. Loss of ADPG1 abolished or strongly reduced PR gene induction in HCT-RNAi and ccr1 backgrounds without restoring lignin composition defects. 3. Water extracts from HCT-RNAi adpg1 lost elicitor activity, and recombinant ADPG1 restored elicitor activity to ccr1 adpg1 extracts, showing ADPG1 is necessary for generating active pectin-derived defense signals.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Microarray transcriptomics; genetic crosses with adpg1 knockout; qRT-PCR of PR genes; recombinant enzyme assay; elicitor bioassays in suspension cells and leaf injection; lignin thioacidolysis; xylem complementation context
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: ADPG1 loss causes defect/delay in anther and silique dehiscence; in HCT-RNAi background partially restores stature but does not restore lignin composition
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ADPG1 is the only pectin-modifying enzyme induced in common between HCT-RNAi and ccr1. Recombinant ADPG1 showed highest activity toward apple pectin and lower activity toward purified PGA, RG-I, and RG-II, suggesting elicitors/precursors may include complex or methylated pectin. Its action appears specific and limited, releasing elicitors without destroying them.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A target for uncoupling lignin engineering from ectopic defense activation; limiting ectopic ADPG1 induction could help maintain biomass improvement while reducing defense-associated growth penalties.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Hydroxycinnamoyl shikimate/quinate intermediates for G and S lignin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers hydroxycinnamoyl groups between CoA thioesters and shikimate/quinate intermediates in the lignin pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor flux to lignin deposition; its reduction indirectly triggers pectin-derived elicitor release
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Secondary cell walls of inflorescence stems
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides secondary wall structure and biomass recalcitrance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Down-regulation reduces lignin and indirectly causes ectopic ADPG1 expression, cell wall remodeling, pectin-derived elicitor release, PR gene activation, and dwarfism
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HCT-RNAi strongly reduced lignin and enriched H units while lowering G and S units. 2. HCT down-regulation caused extensive cell wall remodeling and accumulation of water-extractable pectic elicitors. 3. The defense phenotype in HCT-RNAi required ADPG1.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi line characterization; thioacidolysis; microarray; glycome profiling; elicitor bioassays; genetic interaction with adpg1
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In this paper HCT is a causal upstream lignin-pathway perturbation used to reveal ADPG1-dependent elicitor release, but not the primary focal gene mechanistically dissected in the Results.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Lignin engineering target for reducing recalcitrance, but defense/growth penalties may require co-management of downstream wall-remodeling responses such as ADPG1.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces cinnamoyl-CoA esters to corresponding aldehydes in the penultimate step of monolignol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms lignin precursors; its loss indirectly triggers pectin-derived elicitor release
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Penultimate pathway enzyme; key structural pathway step but not explicitly rate-limiting here
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Loss-of-function mutant in stems; vessel-specific complementation in ProSNBE:CCR1 lines
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Secondary cell walls of inflorescence stems/xylem
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides wall strength, vascular integrity, and biomass recalcitrance
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ccr1-3 and ccr1-6 mutant backgrounds; vessel-specific complementation under ProSNBE
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of CCR1 reduces lignin and induces ectopic ADPG1 expression, latent elicitor release, and PR1 induction; xylem-specific restoration of CCR1 suppresses ADPG1 and PR1 expression
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ccr1 mutants showed reduced lignin, semidwarf growth, and strong induction of ADPG1 and PR genes. 2. ADPG1 loss strongly reduced PR1 induction in the ccr1 background. 3. Xylem-specific complementation with ProSNBE:CCR1 restored lignification in xylem and suppressed ADPG1 and PR1, implicating xylem-origin signals.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; thioacidolysis; microarray; qRT-PCR; genetic crosses with adpg1; vessel-specific complementation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CCR1 is an upstream lignin-pathway perturbation central to the experimental system, but the paper’s main mechanistic focus is ADPG1-dependent release of latent defense elicitors downstream of lignin reduction.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important lignin engineering target; xylem-specific complementation suggests spatial control can mitigate growth/defense penalties.
